title: age-group — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. `#[AsAgeGroupListener]` on a service class marks it as a subscriber to age-group-lifecycle events — AttributeDiscovery picks it up and wires it to the event map declared in `events.json`. No property-level attributes: the AgeGroup row uses ordinary Eloquent + spatie/laravel-data class attributes shipped by foundation.

attributes.name: AsAgeGroupListener

params.name: events
params.description: Array of event short names (`AgeGroupCreated`, `AgeGroupBoundsChanged`, `AgeGroupCatalogSeeded`, …) the class listens to. Empty = subscribe to every event in `Stackra\Sports\AgeGroup\Events\*`. Names are resolved against `events.json`; typos surface at boot time when the discovery scan validates them.

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional queue override. Falls back to the event's default queue (see `events.json`.dispatchMode + per-event `queue`). Use to route long-running listeners onto a dedicated queue.

params.name: priority
params.description: Handler ordering within a single event. Lower runs first. Only meaningful when multiple listeners subscribe to the same event and side-effect ordering matters (e.g. bounds-change audit-log write before downstream Team roster invalidation).
